subject: netconf-pnp-simulator: add IT using ncclient and tox-docker
commit_message:
netconf-pnp-simulator: add IT using ncclient and tox-docker

Integration/functions tests to validate the simulator based on
(Python) ncclient and managed by tox.

Requires v2.6.2

Issue-ID: INT-1124
Change-Id: Id20fa019c444c3ba961ea2d52c20ade86a938c5b
Signed-off-by: ebo <eliezio.oliveira@est.tech>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/ci-management-jjb-verify/4411/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/ci-management-jjb-verify/4411

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> (1 comment)

I'm going to submit the change including the "tox.ini" and other files used by the tests. From your comment on https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/integration/+/102577, I understood that it's better to merge the CI job first. I've just submitted the change here https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/integration/+/103448

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > (1 comment)
> 
> I'm going to submit the change including the "tox.ini" and other files used by the tests. From your comment on https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/integration/+/102577, I understood that it's better to merge the CI job first. I've just submitted the change here https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/integration/+/103448

Correct, but you uploaded code to integration repo after my comment.

In that case I'm not sure that's the correct way of spawning that job, shouldn't you use jjb tox builder with some custom build-script to run tox in the dir where tox.ini is located? I.e. smth similar to:

    builders:
      - lf-infra-tox-install:
          python-version: '{python-version}'
      - lf-infra-tox-run:
          parallel: 'false'

Not sure, just suggesting.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 1:
> > > 
> > > (1 comment)
> > 
> > I'm going to submit the change including the "tox.ini" and other files used by the tests. From your comment on https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/integration/+/102577, I understood that it's better to merge the CI job first. I've just submitted the change here https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/integration/+/103448
> 
> Correct, but you uploaded code to integration repo after my comment.
> 
> In that case I'm not sure that's the correct way of spawning that job, shouldn't you use jjb tox builder with some custom build-script to run tox in the dir where tox.ini is located? I.e. smth similar to:
> 
>     builders:
>       - lf-infra-tox-install:
>           python-version: '{python-version}'
>       - lf-infra-tox-run:
>           parallel: 'false'
> 
> Not sure, just suggesting.

Okay. I wasn't sure what is the idiomatic way to implement this using JJB. What I'm trying to achieve here is to run the integration tests after every docker build. And defining 'post_docker_build_script' seemed to be the most straightforward way. Maybe @jwagantall can help us with this decision.
Uploaded patch set 2: Commit message was updated.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2

I think this approach might work... we can give it a shot
Change has been successfully merged by Jessica Wagantall

From the log messages https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/integration-netconf-pnp-simulator-docker-merge-master/2/console.log.gz it seems that it didn't work as we expected. There is no evidence that tox have even used the 'tox.ini' under the '.../engine' directory.
